summary: Full backend + frontend testing of DocDrift completed. All 8 pytest backend tests passed (health, list repos, RAG chat grounded answer + citations, anti-hallucination fallback, 404/400 guards, drift list, drift status). Frontend UI flow verified end-to-end: app-shell loads, badges db/openai render with 'on' state (github off as expected — no token), ingest-card shows repo URL input with index-btn disabled when empty, clicking expressjs/cors repo-item opens chat-view, chat-input + send-btn produce an assistant answer with grounded markdown code block and citation chips (cite-1 with valid github blob URL), drift tab shows drift-view with 2 drift-flag cards (medium severity) and rescan-btn present.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: CORSMiddleware added AFTER include_router — works in Starlette but conventionally middleware is added before mounting. No functional bug observed.
rag.py: _client() is instantiated on every embed/completion call; could be cached at module level for a small perf win.
No rate-limiting on /api/repos POST — a malicious client could exhaust GitHub/OpenAI quotas. Acceptable for portfolio scope.
seed_data_creation: None — used already-indexed expressjs/cors repo per instructions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (pytest, uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). Two repos pre-indexed: expressjs/cors and Tejalpatel2099/Mockup-Interview-coach. Do NOT trigger new ingestion (unauthenticated GitHub rate limit). Chat citations render as [data-testid=cite-N] anchors. Health badge state is encoded via CSS class 'on' (not text) — assert on class attribute, not text_content.
